mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding real economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings and supports higher equity prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of expansion months over a rolling 3-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals imminent consumer weakness and possible recession, leading to negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses above 5% the SPX 60-day return is positive in more than 40% of occurrences over a 5-year walk-forward, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment alongside accelerating payroll growth indicates an overheating labor market that forces the Fed to tighten, generating negative equity returns.
failure_condition: If the joint signal of declining UNRATE and rising PAYEMS is followed by positive SPX 20-day returns in more than half of occurrences over any 3-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed funds rate is low, rising housing starts signal a vibrant construction sector and consumer confidence, boosting equity prices over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If in the low-rate regime, rising HOUST is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more than 40% of the time over a 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales exceeding 1% signals a sudden drop in consumer spending, prompting negative earnings revisions and downward equity pressure within one month.
failure_condition: If after a >1% monthly drop in RSAFS, the SPX 20-day return is positive in over 50% of instances across a 3-year walk-forward, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in consumer price inflation above 3% year-over-year forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and leading to negative returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If after CPI Y/Y exceeds 3%, SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggers across a 5-year window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth outpaces industrial production growth, demand exceeds supply capacity, generating cost-push inflation and margin compression that weighs on equity returns over the long horizon.
failure_condition: If the joint signal of GDPC1 growth exceeding INDPRO growth is followed by positive SPX 60-day returns in more than half the occurrences over any 3-year rolling period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll gains above 180,000 indicate robust labor demand and rising household incomes, supporting consumer discretionary spending and positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: If after payroll gains exceed 180k, SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 40% of instances over a 3-year walk-forward, the hypothesis is falsified.
